tactical.bear_case: RSI 62 after a sharp multi-week rally into freight rate weakness — spot rates (Drewry WCI) have been rolling over from 2024 Red Sea highs, and a -4% candle can mark the start of mean reversion back toward the SMA50 at $12.16, a ~11% downside gap.
tactical.bull_case: The -4% is a single liquidity-driven ADR move; underlying Copenhagen shares plus a still-golden-cross structure and price holding above both MAs suggests dip-buyers defend $13, with any Suez re-escalation spiking rates and the tape instantly.
tactical.falsifier: Drewry WCI composite turning back UP >10% week-over-week (Suez/Red Sea re-disruption) would flip this to a buy-lean as rates re-accelerate.

positional.bear_case: Massive container newbuild deliveries in 2025-26 expand effective capacity just as Red Sea reroute demand fades, pressuring spot rates and earnings — the golden cross becomes a lagging artifact of a peak already passed.
positional.bull_case: Maersk's integrator logistics strategy and disciplined Gemini network with Hapag-Lloyd could sustain above-trend earnings; sustained price above SMA50/200 reflects a market re-rating the franchise, not just spot rates.
positional.falsifier: A decisive close below the SMA50 ($12.16) breaking the golden-cross structure would flip the positional lean to buy-side (oversold value) rather than trim.

strategic.bear_case: If 2025-26 is actually a cyclical TOP disguised by Red Sea-inflated rates, then 25x P/E is masking peak-ish earnings and reverts toward trough; record orderbook deliveries crush rates into 2026, and 0.71x P/B can fall further toward 0.4-0.5x as it did in prior down-cycles (2016, 2019).
strategic.bull_case: Below-replacement P/B with logistics priced at zero is a multi-year value setup; capital returns (buybacks/dividends), Gemini network cost savings, and any structural Suez avoidance keeping rates elevated drive a re-rating toward book and beyond.
strategic.falsifier: P/B re-rating above ~1.0x (signaling earnings/cycle peak being priced) OR a sustained freight rate collapse with WCI falling below pre-2024 baseline would flip the strategic sign to trim.

tactical.bear_case: 23% above SMA50 with RSI near 68 after a vertical move; any disappointment in semicap commentary or a China export headline could trigger a fast 5-8% pullback from these stretched levels.
tactical.bull_case: EUV/High-NA demand momentum is real and order flow could keep pushing the stock; momentum names like ASML routinely run RSI 70+ for weeks without reverting, so fading strength here loses.
tactical.falsifier: A clean break and hold above recent highs on accelerating volume (or a strong upside order/booking pre-announcement) would flip this to a buy-side tactical read.

positional.bear_case: The price-to-SMA50 gap of 23% is historically wide; reversion to the 50-day alone implies ~18% downside, and consolidations after such runs are common.
positional.bull_case: Backlog visibility of 2-3 years and High-NA ramp give fundamental support; the trend has been a reliable up-and-to-the-right channel and dips have been shallow and quickly bought.
positional.falsifier: A pullback of >10% toward the SMA50 that holds, or a backlog/book-to-bill acceleration, would shift positional to neutral-to-buy.

strategic.bear_case: If 2026 EPS recovery disappoints (China export controls tighten further, memory/logic capex digestion lingers), the elevated multiple compresses and the stock could derate 15-20% even with intact long-term story.
strategic.bull_case: Sole EUV supplier with widening High-NA lead ($350M+/tool), AI-driven leading-edge demand, and durable double-digit growth; at this scarcity premium the multiple can stay elevated and compound, making any neutral-to-trim score wrong.
strategic.falsifier: Forward P/E falling below ~25x (via EPS beats or a >15% drawdown) would flip strategic decisively to the buy side; conversely forward P/E pushing above 45x on flat estimates would justify a stronger trim.

tactical.bear_case: ENB has fallen two consecutive sessions (56.85→55.93) and a break of the 54.8 SMA50 could accelerate selling if the 10Y keeps creeping toward 4.5%+, making the thin 46bps yield spread look even worse.
tactical.bull_case: A modest pullback near the rising SMA50 is a classic support bounce setup for an income name; any soft inflation print or dovish Fed/BoC comment could spark a sympathetic rate-driven rally given ENB's bond proxy behavior.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive RSI move below 35 (oversold on a rate spike) would flip this to a buy; an RSI surge above 70 on no fundamental change would flip it more positive.

positional.bear_case: 46bps spread is near the bottom of ENB's historical range — when the 10Y is paying you almost as much as the pipeline equity with full downside risk, the relative-value case for trimming is strong, especially with 14 straight TRIM days validated by yesterday's drop.
positional.bull_case: The thin spread is partly a function of the 10Y being elevated, not ENB being expensive in absolute terms; if BoC/Fed begin cutting, the 10Y falls and ENB re-rates higher fast. LNG Canada Phase 1 first cargoes and Phase 2 sanctioning are near-term volume catalysts that the spread metric ignores entirely.
positional.falsifier: Yield spread widening back above ~150bps — whether via an ENB price drop or a 10Y decline to ~3.4% — would flip this to a buy. A dividend growth guidance raise on LNG/gas volume strength would also turn the sign negative.

strategic.bear_case: With real yields at 2.16% and the curve only +40bps, a bond-proxy paying 4.9% at 25x earnings offers a thin long-run risk premium; capital is better deployed elsewhere in the book until the spread normalizes, and pipeline permitting/regulatory risk caps multiple expansion.
strategic.bull_case: ENB's LNG Canada and Gulf Coast gas infrastructure positions it for a structural multi-year volume tailwind as global LNG demand grows; the franchise is effectively un-replicable and the dividend has compounded for ~29 years. A rate-cutting cycle would re-rate the entire midstream-as-income complex.
strategic.falsifier: Start of a sustained BoC/Fed easing cycle (10Y trending below 3.5%) or LNG Canada Phase 2 sanctioning with raised dividend-growth guidance would flip this negative; a dividend coverage deterioration or major pipeline obsolescence event would push it more positive.

strategic.bear_case: Real yields at 2.24% and a compressed 5.11% dividend yield mean the income thesis is expensive; if rates stay higher-for-longer, ENB de-rates and total return lags the book's growthier compounders (MSFT/NOW/ASML).
strategic.bull_case: LNG Canada Phase 2 and Gulf Coast gas expansion plus de-risked utility cash flows underwrite mid-single-digit DCF growth; when the rate cycle turns, this becomes a top-quartile total-return income name with a rising dividend.
strategic.falsifier: A dividend-growth guidance cut, a Mainline recontracting/volume shock, or 10Y falling below ~4% (making the 5.11% yield attractive again) would flip the strategic sign.

tactical.falsifier: A close below the 200-day SMA ($12.57) on rising volume would flip this positive (buy) into a trim/avoid tactical read.

positional.bear_case: GLNCY outperforming COPX means the copper upside optionality is now less discounted — mean reversion could see GLNCY give back the 6.4pp premium if the trading arm's trough EBITDA guidance disappoints; below-50-day trend means momentum is not yet confirmed.
positional.bull_case: Below-50/above-200 is the classic buy zone; with copper structurally supply-constrained and Glencore's coal cash flows funding buybacks, a reclaim of the 50-day ($15.08) opens meaningful upside and the diversification premium keeps expanding.
positional.falsifier: GLNCY reversing to UNDERPERFORM COPX by a widening margin (premium turning to discount) plus HY OAS widening past ~3.5bps would flip the positional sign to trim.

strategic.bear_case: P/B 1.64 is NOT below replacement, so the deep-value floor argument is weaker than a sub-0.8 P/B name; if Chinese property stays weak and copper stalls, the earnings trough deepens further and the 223x P/E is trough for a reason, not a spring — coal ESG divestment pressure adds a structural derating overhang.
strategic.bull_case: Trough earnings + supply-constrained copper + coal cash cow funding aggressive buybacks = textbook cyclical bottom; when copper turns, Glencore's operating leverage plus trading-arm optionality drives multi-bagger EPS recovery that the 223x P/E completely masks today.
strategic.falsifier: LME copper breaking decisively below key support with rising inventories AND Chinese PMI printing sub-48 would signal the trough is deepening, flipping strategic to neutral/trim.

strategic.bull_case: Structural ETF-driven demand is de-linking BTC from the mechanical halving cycle; buying a scarce store-of-value 25% below its 200DMA with real rates likely peaking sets up strong multi-year returns.
strategic.falsifier: Clear evidence the halving cycle is topping (LTH mass distribution + sustained multi-month ETF outflows) would flip strategic to a trim tilt.

tactical.bear_case: If today's drop is the leading edge of an EM risk-off (peso wobble, US rate scare), a calm RSI understates near-term downside as the ADR gaps lower.
